name: cairn
description: Token-efficient agent harness with tool readiness for Codex, Claude Code, and Antigravity.

interface.shortDescription: Memory, planning, tool readiness, and two-gate verification harness for agent teams.
interface.longDescription: Cairn brings a LazyCodex-inspired flow to Codex, Claude Code, and Antigravity while reducing token use through repository-local MEMORY.md, PLAN.md, domain notes, tool readiness checks, module-task execution, and two-gate verification.
defaultPrompt: Use Cairn to create MEMORY.md and PLAN.md for this repository.
Every agent must read the project-root MEMORY.md before planning, implementation, review, or delegation. Keep MEMORY.md and PLAN.md as short indexes; open only current-task details.
Resolve Cairn runtime resources only through the installed locator, keep repoRoot separate, and run toolcheck before implementation planning. Tool installation requires explicit approval.

Run cairn-plan before non-trivial changes. Understand the whole work, split it into executable tasks/sub-tasks, record Light/Heavy Path triage, and finalize a decision-complete implementation plan before editing.
The user-called/main agent is the orchestrator. Delegate bounded implementation edits to worker subagents when allowed; otherwise the main agent implements and records takeover evidence.
With a progress-reporting channel, subagents report start, direction, periodic progress, and finish; relay those events immediately. Otherwise relay assignment, waiting, and final completion.

Delegated agents read MEMORY.md, preserve others' edits, return a final report, and may recursively delegate only bounded work. Capture evidence, close/release the agent, then review its report before completion.

Implementation/continue requests authorize an active Cairn goal even without the word goal. After MEMORY.md, write an initial plan with triage-plan active; before exploration call update_plan, then create_goal, then start/attach the repository goal. Triage finalizes the repository and UI plans before implementation. Keep roadmap/current-task recovery context; consultation, explanation, and plan-only requests stay goal-free.

Use a test contract first: spend reasoning on requirements, invariants, boundaries, and failure modes; design executable tests before implementation; make the minimum implementation that passes. Treat tool exit codes and machine summaries as authoritative, and expand context only for failing tests.

Missing, failed, skipped, stale, or placeholder evidence never completes work. Advance a UI step only after the matching repository task passes bound evidence; workers report only assigned tasks; active is not complete.

Use goal verify -- <argv>, quiet state mutations, and concise success summaries. Run focused tests and one final full check. Inspect package lifecycle scripts before packaging: run normal npm pack --dry-run by default; never skip content-producing or unknown scripts with --ignore-scripts, and use it only for absent or proven content-neutral scripts while evidence is fresh.
If blocked, pause/block/cancel explicitly. Answer side questions briefly, then resume the current task unless asked to pause, stop, or switch.
